title: CLI: task tracker with JSON storage
prompt: Build a command-line task tracker as a Node.js ESM executable (no npm dependencies). The executable must live at bin/task.mjs so `node bin/task.mjs <command>` works from the workspace root. Support `task add "title"`, `task list`, `task done <id>`, and `task remove <id>`, storing tasks in a JSON file whose path can be overridden with the TASKS_FILE environment variable. Invalid commands, missing arguments, and unknown ids must print a clear error to stderr and exit non-zero, while successful commands exit zero with readable output (list output must include each task's title). Cover every command and every error path with node --test tests that invoke the CLI as a child process against a temp TASKS_FILE.

objectiveChecks.command: rm -f .probe-tasks.json && TASKS_FILE=.probe-tasks.json node bin/task.mjs add 'probe item' && TASKS_FILE=.probe-tasks.json node bin/task.mjs list | grep -i 'probe item' >/dev/null && test -s .probe-tasks.json

rm -f .probe-tasks.json && TASKS_FILE=.probe-tasks.json node bin/task.mjs add 'x' >/dev/null && if TASKS_FILE=.probe-tasks.json node bin/task.mjs done 999999 2>.probe-err.txt; then exit 1; fi && test -s .probe-err.txt
if node bin/task.mjs frobnicate 2>.probe-err2.txt; then exit 1; fi && test -s .probe-err2.txt

judgeFocus: exit-code and stderr discipline on every error path
tests drive the real CLI as a child process, not internal functions only
TASKS_FILE override works and tests use it for isolation

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: All four commands must work end to end through the executable at bin/task.mjs.
Every error path must exit non-zero with a clear stderr message.
TASKS_FILE must override the storage location.
The fixture baseline test must remain green.
All probe-* objective checks must pass; they exercise the real CLI.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace implements the commands, storage override, and error behavior described in the prompt.
Whether tests pass, exit codes and streams are used correctly, and state transitions (add/done/remove) behave without regressions.
Maintainability, clean argument parsing, small focused diff, and child-process test coverage of real CLI behavior.
Quality of planning artifacts, contract specificity, and repair-loop behavior relative to the ceremony level used.
Whether verdicts are backed by reproducible command evidence rather than claims.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Tests that only import internal functions without exercising the executable should cap correctness at 3.
Prefer concrete test output evidence over implementation claims.

notes: Part of the ceremony-ladder benchmark suite. Designed to compare ceremony levels and providers on identical CLI work.
